easy.companyDescription: A subscription entertainment company trying to move more of its customer experience online.
easy.macroContext: Broadband use, connected devices, and paid digital media habits are all expanding.
easy.situation: Can a newer delivery model rebuild trust quickly enough to justify rising content and product spending?
easy.longCase: If customers accept the newer model, recurring revenue can scale with broadband and connected-device adoption.
easy.shortCase: A recent trust shock can keep churn elevated while content commitments rise faster than the subscriber base.
setupHints: The old delivery model still contributes cash, but the newer model is where the growth case lives.

medium.companyDescription: A consumer media company with a recurring billing model.
medium.macroContext: Households are spending more leisure time on connected screens.
medium.situation: Can engagement recover fast enough to support rising content and product costs?
medium.longCase: If habits stick, recurring revenue can compound as more households go digital.
medium.shortCase: Elevated churn and fixed content commitments can pressure the model before scale arrives.

hard.macroContext: Digital consumption is rising, but winners among media models are still unclear.
hard.situation: Will customer loyalty return before spending outruns the base?
hard.longCase: Habit formation at scale can turn a recurring model into a compounding franchise.
hard.shortCase: If customers stay skeptical, rising fixed costs can overwhelm growth.

reveal.shortText: That was Netflix. The market was skeptical, but streaming adoption accelerated.
reveal.funFact: The company's transition looked risky at the time, but it became one of the defining consumer-tech winners of the decade.
whyItMoved: Subscriber growth improved after the trust reset.
The company became a stronger platform business over time.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: Medium/Hard rewritten after H023 Gate 2 identity failures (controversial transition + distribution shift + post-drawdown triangulation). Intended peer set: Netflix / Sirius XM / Pandora / Dish / Comcast / Time Warner-class media names. Easy unchanged; Medium/Hard Gate 2 pending re-judge.

guesses.pointingFact: Jan 2012 subscription entertainment firm moving customers online after a trust shock while old delivery still contributes cash
Subscription media model scaling with broadband and connected devices
Recurring-revenue entertainment business navigating a delivery-model shift
Entertainment distributor with old delivery cash flows and newer digital growth questions
Online entertainment subscription effort competing for household habit formation

direction.cue: If customers accept the newer model, recurring revenue can scale with broadband adoption after the stock reset
